results.rendered_html: hiṃsā f. injury, harm (to life or property), hurt, mischief, wrong (said to be of three kinds, 1. mental as ‘bearing malice’; 2. verbal, as ‘abusive language’; 3. personal, as ‘acts of violence’),  Mn. ;  Yājñ. ;  MBh.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
hiṃsā [hiṃs-a]
1 Injury, mischief, wrong, harm, hurt (said to be of three kinds:-kāyika ‘personal’, vācika ‘verbal’, and mānasika ‘mental’); ahiṃsā paramo dharmaḥ.
2 Killing, slaying, destruction;  R. 5. 57 ; 3. 313 ;  Ms. 10. 63. 
3 Robbery, plunder.
Comp.
— ātmaka a. injurious, destructive.
— karman n.
1 any hurtful or injurious act.
2 magic used to effect the ruin or injury of an enemy (= abhicāra q. v.).
— prāṇin m. a noxious animal.
— rata a. delighting
(pb) in mischief.
— ruci a, intent on or delighting in mischief;  Māl. 5. 29 ,
— samudbhava a, arising from injury.
